description: my-skills - AI Agent Skills Manager CLI and Web App. Backwards-compatible replacement for npx skills with multi-tool support, web UI, composition builder, and favorites system.

userStories.title: Scaffold monorepo with create-t3-turbo
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the monorepo scaffolded so that all packages can be developed and built together.
acceptanceCriteria: Run `npx create-turbo@latest -e https://github.com/t3-oss/create-t3-turbo --name my-skills` with non-interactive flags in a temp directory, then move contents into this repo
Rename namespace from @acme/ to @curiouslycory/ across all packages using find-and-replace
Root pnpm-workspace.yaml includes apps/*, packages/*, tooling/*
Root turbo.json defines task pipeline: build, dev, typecheck, lint, test, clean
Remove the expo and tanstack-start apps (keep only nextjs, renamed to web)
Create empty skills/ and artifacts/{agents,prompts,claudemds,compositions} directories with .gitkeep files
.gitignore covers node_modules/, dist/, *.db, .env, .my-skills/
pnpm install succeeds from root
pnpm build succeeds (or at minimum pnpm typecheck passes)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the foundation. create-t3-turbo includes: Next.js app, packages (api, auth, db, ui), and tooling (eslint, prettier, tailwind, typescript). The nextjs app should be renamed to 'web' in its directory and package.json. Preserve the existing .claude/ and .agents/ directories - do NOT delete them.

userStories.title: Create shared-types package with core Zod schemas
userStories.description: As a developer, I need shared TypeScript types and Zod schemas so the CLI and web app use consistent definitions.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/shared-types/ exists as @curiouslycory/shared-types with package.json, tsconfig.json extending @curiouslycory/tsconfig
Exports ArtifactCategory type: 'skill' | 'agent' | 'prompt' | 'claudemd'
Exports AgentId type: 'claude-code' | 'cursor' | 'cline' | 'warp' | 'amp' | 'opencode' | 'github-copilot' | 'codex' | 'gemini-cli' | 'kimi-code'
Exports SkillFrontmatter interface: name, description, license?, compatibility?, metadata?, allowed-tools?
Exports ManifestSchema Zod schema: version (literal 1), agents (string array), skills (record of SkillEntrySchema)
Exports SkillEntrySchema: source (string), sourceType (enum github/gitlab/url/local), computedHash, version?, installedAt, agents?, variations?
Exports ConfigSchema: defaultAgents[], favoriteRepos[], cacheDir, skillsDir, autoDetectAgents, symlinkBehavior
Exports CATEGORY_DIR_MAP: skill->skills, agent->agents, prompt->prompts, claudemd->claudemds
Exports DEPLOY_PATH_MAP: skill->.agents/skills, agent->.agents/agents, prompt->.agents/prompts, claudemd->.
Exports AGENT_NATIVE_SUPPORT: map of which agents read .agents/skills/ natively (claude-code, cline, cursor, warp, amp, opencode -> true)
Package builds successfully
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This package is dependency-free (only zod). Other packages will import from it. Use zod .infer<> for TypeScript types derived from schemas.

userStories.title: Create shared-types frontmatter utilities
userStories.description: As a developer, I need frontmatter parsing utilities so skills can be read and written consistently.
acceptanceCriteria: Add gray-matter as a dependency to @curiouslycory/shared-types
Exports parseSkillFrontmatter(content: string): { frontmatter: SkillFrontmatter, body: string }
Exports buildSkillContent(frontmatter: SkillFrontmatter, body: string): string that produces valid SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter
parseSkillFrontmatter correctly handles SKILL.md files with --- delimited YAML frontmatter
Round-trip: buildSkillContent(parseSkillFrontmatter(content)) preserves content semantically
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use gray-matter library for parsing. The frontmatter format follows the agent skills spec: --- delimited YAML with name and description as required fields.

userStories.title: Create git-service package
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a shared git abstraction so both the web app and CLI use consistent git operations.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/git-service/ exists as @curiouslycory/git-service with package.json

GitService class wraps simple-git with methods: clone(url, path, opts), fetch(opts), pull(), status(), log(filePath?, maxCount?), diff(filePath?, fromCommit?, toCommit?), commit(files, message), push(), branches(), checkout(ref), showFileAtCommit(filePath, commitHash)
clone() supports { depth: 1 } option for shallow clones
Constructor accepts repoPath parameter
All methods have proper TypeScript types for inputs and return values
Package builds successfully
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use simple-git npm package. This is used by both the CLI (for cache management) and the web app (for git UI operations).

userStories.title: Create database package with Drizzle schema
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the database schema so the web app can store and search skill data.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/db/ exists as @curiouslycory/db (modify existing create-t3-turbo db package)
Replace Supabase/Postgres with SQLite via better-sqlite3 and drizzle-orm/better-sqlite3

skills table: id (text PK, UUID default), name (text, unique), description (text), tags (text, JSON array), author (text), version (text), content (text), dirPath (text, unique), category (text), createdAt (integer timestamp), updatedAt (integer timestamp)
variations table: id (text PK), name (text), description (text), tags (text), content (text), filePath (text), skillId (text FK -> skills.id, cascade delete)
favorites table: id (text PK), repoUrl (text, unique), name (text), description (text), addedAt (integer timestamp)
compositions table: id (text PK), name (text), description (text), fragments (text, JSON), order (text, JSON), createdAt (integer timestamp), updatedAt (integer timestamp)
config table: id (text PK), key (text, unique), value (text)
DB_DIALECT env var support in client.ts (sqlite default, with seam for turso/postgres future expansion)
DB_PATH env var for SQLite file location (default: ./data/my-skills.db)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Modify the existing @acme/db package from create-t3-turbo. Replace the Supabase/Postgres setup with SQLite. Keep the adapter pattern seam by reading DB_DIALECT but only implement sqlite for now. Add data/ to .gitignore.

userStories.title: Set up FTS5 virtual table for full-text search
userStories.description: As a developer, I need FTS5 so the web app can perform fast full-text search across skills.
acceptanceCriteria: Create packages/db/src/fts.ts with initFTS(db) function
Creates FTS5 virtual table skills_fts with columns: name, description, tags, content using CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS
Creates AFTER INSERT trigger on skills table to insert into skills_fts
Creates AFTER UPDATE trigger on skills table to update skills_fts
Creates AFTER DELETE trigger on skills table to delete from skills_fts
Export initFTS function from package
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: FTS5 is SQLite-specific. Use db.run(sql`...`) with raw SQL since Drizzle cannot model virtual tables. The triggers use content= syntax to keep FTS in sync automatically.

userStories.title: Scaffold CLI with Commander
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the CLI scaffolded with Commander.js so I can add commands incrementally.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/cli/ exists with package.json: name 'my-skills', type 'module', engines node >=18
bin field registers both 'my-skills' and 'ms' pointing to dist/bin/my-skills.js
tsup.config.ts configured: ESM output, node18 target, entry src/bin/my-skills.ts with shebang banner
src/bin/my-skills.ts creates Commander program with name('my-skills'), version from package.json, description
src/program.ts exports the program creation function
Dependencies: commander, chalk, ora, @inquirer/search, @inquirer/checkbox, gray-matter
Workspace dependencies: @curiouslycory/shared-types, @curiouslycory/git-service
pnpm build --filter my-skills produces dist/bin/my-skills.js with shebang
Vitest configured in vitest.config.ts
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The CLI is a separate app in apps/cli/. It uses tsup for bundling. The bin entry should have #!/usr/bin/env node shebang via tsup banner option.

userStories.title: Implement CLI config system
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the global config module so the CLI can store and retrieve user preferences.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/cli/src/core/config.ts
loadConfig() reads ~/.my-skills/config.json, validates with ConfigSchema from @curiouslycory/shared-types, returns config with defaults for missing fields
saveConfig(config) writes atomically: write to .tmp file then rename
Creates ~/.my-skills/ directory if it doesn't exist (using fs.mkdir recursive)
Environment variables MY_SKILLS_CACHE_DIR, MY_SKILLS_SKILLS_DIR override file config values
Returns sensible defaults when config file doesn't exist: favoriteRepos defaults to empty array, cacheDir defaults to ~/.my-skills/cache, skillsDir defaults to .agents/skills
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use node:fs/promises and node:os (homedir). The config file is the bridge between CLI and web app.

userStories.title: Implement CLI manifest module
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the manifest module so the CLI can track which skills are installed in a project.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/cli/src/core/manifest.ts
loadManifest(projectRoot) reads .my-skills.json, validates with ManifestSchema, returns null if file missing
saveManifest(projectRoot, manifest) writes atomically with pretty-printed JSON (2-space indent)
addSkill(manifest, name, entry) returns new manifest with skill added (pure function)
removeSkill(manifest, name) returns new manifest with skill removed (pure function)
getSkill(manifest, name) returns SkillManifestEntry or undefined
Create apps/cli/src/core/skill-hasher.ts with computeSkillHash(dirPath) that computes SHA-256 from all files in directory sorted alphabetically for determinism
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use node:crypto for SHA-256. The manifest is .my-skills.json in the project root. Pure functions make testing easy.

userStories.title: Implement source parser
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to parse skill source strings into structured objects so the CLI can resolve where to fetch skills from.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/cli/src/services/source-parser.ts
parseSource('owner/repo') returns { type: 'github', owner: 'owner', repo: 'repo', skill: undefined, url: 'https://github.com/owner/repo.git' }
parseSource('owner/repo/skill-name') returns { type: 'github', owner, repo, skill: 'skill-name', url }
parseSource('https://github.com/owner/repo') returns { type: 'github', owner, repo, url }
parseSource('https://github.com/owner/repo.git') also works
parseSource('./local/path') returns { type: 'local', path: absoluteResolvedPath }
Throws descriptive error for unparseable sources
Export SkillSource union type
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: GitHub URL parsing should handle both https://github.com/owner/repo and https://github.com/owner/repo.git formats. The owner/repo shorthand always assumes GitHub.

userStories.title: Implement cache service for remote repos
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the cache service so the CLI can maintain shallow git clones of remote skill repositories.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/cli/src/services/cache.ts
getCachePath(source) returns ~/.my-skills/cache/<owner>/<repo>/
fetchRepo(source) does shallow clone (depth 1) on first fetch using @curiouslycory/git-service
fetchRepo(source) does git fetch --depth 1 + git reset --hard origin/HEAD on subsequent fetches
Stores .my-skills-cache-meta.json with { lastFetched: ISO8601 } in each cache dir
isCacheStale(source, ttlMs) compares lastFetched against TTL (default 1 hour)
getCachedRepoPath(source) returns path if cached, null if not
discoverSkills(repoPath) walks repo for SKILL.md files, parses frontmatter, returns array of { name, description, path }
Creates ~/.my-skills/cache/ directory if needed
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: discoverSkills should search: root SKILL.md, skills/*/SKILL.md, */SKILL.md, and deeper nested directories. Use recursive directory walking with fs.readdir.

userStories.title: Implement skill resolver and installer
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the skill resolver to locate skills in cached repos and the installer to deploy them.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/cli/src/core/skill-resolver.ts with resolveSkill(source, skillName, cachePath) that returns { name, sourcePath, frontmatter, content, files[] }
Resolver searches cached repo for matching skill by name in SKILL.md frontmatter
Create apps/cli/src/core/skill-installer.ts with installSkill(skill, targetDir) that copies the entire skill directory to targetDir/<skill-name>/
Installer creates parent directories if needed using fs.mkdir recursive
Installer returns the computed content hash of the installed skill
Installer copies all files in the skill directory (SKILL.md + any supporting files like scripts/, references/, assets/)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The resolver reads from the cache, the installer writes to the project. They are separate concerns. Use fs.cp with recursive option for directory copying.

userStories.title: Implement skills-lock.json migration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need migration logic so projects using skills.sh can seamlessly transition to my-skills.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/cli/src/core/migration.ts
migrateFromSkillsLock(projectRoot) detects skills-lock.json when .my-skills.json is missing
Transforms skills-lock format: copies source, sourceType, computedHash; adds installedAt as current ISO timestamp; sets version to 1, agents to empty array
Writes .my-skills.json with migrated data
Does NOT delete skills-lock.json (preserves for backwards compat)
Returns the migrated manifest object
Returns null if no skills-lock.json found
Prints informative message: 'Migrated skills-lock.json -> .my-skills.json'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The skills-lock.json format: { version: 1, skills: { 'skill-name': { source, sourceType, computedHash } } }. See existing skills-lock.json in project root for reference.

userStories.title: Implement add command - core install from repo
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the core add command that installs a skill from a GitHub repository.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/cli/src/commands/add.ts registered on the Commander program with aliases 'a', 'i', 'install'
ms add owner/repo/skill-name: parses source, fetches repo to cache, resolves skill, installs to .agents/skills/<name>/, updates .my-skills.json manifest
ms add owner/repo (no skill name): fetches repo, discovers skills, presents @inquirer/search interactive picker showing name + description, then installs selected skill
Shows ora spinner during fetch and install operations
Shows chalk success message with skill name and target path after install
Shows error message if skill not found in repo
Checks manifest first - if skill already installed, warns and suggests 'ms update' instead
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the core add flow without flags. Use source-parser, cache service, skill-resolver, skill-installer, and manifest module. The interactive picker uses @inquirer/search.

userStories.title: Implement add command - restore from manifest
userStories.description: As a developer, I want ms add with no arguments to install all skills from the manifest, like npm install.
acceptanceCriteria: ms add (no arguments) reads .my-skills.json manifest
If no manifest found, checks for skills-lock.json and migrates first
For each skill entry: resolves source via cache, installs if not already present locally
Skips skills already installed with matching hash (prints 'already up to date')
Shows summary: N installed, N already up-to-date, N failed
Shows per-skill errors for missing repos or invalid sources
If manifest is empty or missing, prints helpful message about usage
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This enables the npm install workflow: clone a repo, run ms add, and all skills from the manifest are installed.

userStories.title: Implement add command flags
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the add command flags for controlling installation behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: --skill <names> flag: comma-separated skill names to install without interactive picker
--agent <agents> flag: comma-separated agent targets to override defaults
-y / --yes flag: skip all confirmation prompts
-g / --global flag: install to ~/.agents/skills/ instead of project .agents/skills/
--copy flag: copy files instead of relying on canonical location (for environments without symlink support)
--all flag: shorthand for --skill '*' --agent '*' -y
-l / --list flag: list available skills in the repo without installing
All flags work correctly with both owner/repo and owner/repo/skill-name source formats
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Flags modify the behavior of the core add flow from US-014. --list just runs discoverSkills and prints the table without installing.

userStories.title: Implement remove command
userStories.description: As a developer, I want ms remove to uninstall skills from a project.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/cli/src/commands/remove.ts registered with aliases 'r', 'uninstall'
ms remove <skill-name>: removes .agents/skills/<name>/ directory, removes entry from .my-skills.json
Prompts for confirmation before deleting (unless -y flag)
ms remove (no args): shows interactive list of installed skills from manifest for selection
Supports --skill <names> for removing multiple skills
Supports -g / --global to remove from ~/.agents/skills/
Supports -y / --yes to skip confirmation
Supports --all shorthand
Shows error if skill is not installed
Shows success message after removal
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use fs.rm with recursive and force options. Read manifest to verify skill is installed before attempting removal.

userStories.title: Implement list command
userStories.description: As a developer, I want ms list to see all installed skills.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/cli/src/commands/list.ts registered with alias 'ls'
ms list reads .my-skills.json and displays installed skills as formatted table using chalk
Table columns: name, source, version (or hash truncated to 8 chars)
--global flag lists globally installed skills from ~/.agents/skills/
--agent <name> flag filters to skills targeting a specific agent
--json flag outputs machine-readable JSON array (no ANSI codes)
Shows 'No skills installed' if manifest is empty or missing
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: For --global, scan ~/.agents/skills/ directories and read SKILL.md frontmatter since there may not be a global manifest.

userStories.title: Implement adapter interface and registry
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the adapter system so skills can be deployed to different AI coding tools.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/cli/src/adapters/types.ts with AgentAdapter interface: id (AgentId), displayName (string), detect(projectRoot): Promise<boolean>, install(projectRoot, skill): Promise<void>, remove(projectRoot, skillName): Promise<void>, sync(projectRoot, skills): Promise<void>, getSkillsPath(projectRoot): string
Create SkillEntry type: name, sourcePath, frontmatter (SkillFrontmatter), content (string), files (string[])
Create apps/cli/src/adapters/registry.ts with adapterRegistry Map<AgentId, AgentAdapter>
Export getAdapter(id) and getEnabledAdapters(agents) functions
All 10 agent IDs from AgentId type have an adapter registered (even if some are initially NativeAdapter)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The registry is the central lookup for all adapters. Initially all agents can use NativeAdapter, then we'll add specialized adapters in subsequent stories.

userStories.title: Implement agent detection
userStories.description: As a developer, I need auto-detection so the CLI discovers which AI tools are present in a project.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/cli/src/adapters/detect.ts with detectAgents(projectRoot): Promise<AgentId[]>

Checks for: .claude/ or CLAUDE.md (claude-code), .cursor/ or .cursorrules (cursor), .cline/ or .clinerules (cline), .warp/ (warp), .amp/ or AGENTS.md (amp), .opencode/ (opencode), .github/copilot-instructions.md (github-copilot), .codex/ (codex), .gemini/ or GEMINI.md (gemini-cli), .kimi/ (kimi-code)
On first run (no agents in manifest or config): presents @inquirer/checkbox with detected agents pre-selected
Saves selection to manifest agents array and global config defaultAgents
Prints info: 'Run `ms config set defaultAgents ...` to change defaults'
Subsequent runs use saved config without prompting
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use fs.access or fs.stat to check for directory/file existence. The checkbox prompt allows users to select/deselect any detected agents.

userStories.title: Implement NativeAdapter and SymlinkAdapter
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the native and symlink adapters for the majority of AI tools.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/cli/src/adapters/native.ts: NativeAdapter where install/remove/sync are no-ops (tools read .agents/skills/ directly)
NativeAdapter used for: claude-code, cline, cursor, warp, amp, opencode (check AGENT_NATIVE_SUPPORT from shared-types)
Create apps/cli/src/adapters/symlink.ts: SymlinkAdapter creates/removes symlinks from tool-specific dirs to .agents/skills/<name>/
Symlink paths configurable per agent: .claude/skills/, .cursor/skills/, etc.
SymlinkAdapter.install creates symlink and parent directory if needed
SymlinkAdapter.remove removes the symlink (not the target)
Symlink adapter only used when user explicitly configures symlinkBehavior in config
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use fs.symlink for creating symlinks, fs.unlink for removing. On Windows, consider using fs.symlink with 'junction' type as fallback.

userStories.title: Implement CopilotAdapter
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Copilot adapter to manage skill content in copilot-instructions.md.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/cli/src/adapters/copilot.ts implementing AgentAdapter
install() adds skill content between <!-- my-skills:skill-name:start --> and <!-- my-skills:skill-name:end --> markers in .github/copilot-instructions.md
All managed content is wrapped in outer <!-- my-skills:start --> and <!-- my-skills:end --> markers
remove() removes the skill's section (its start/end markers and content between them)
Preserves all content outside the managed markers
Creates .github/copilot-instructions.md with markers if file doesn't exist
Creates .github/ directory if needed
sync() replaces entire managed section with all current skills
detect() checks for .github/copilot-instructions.md or .github/ directory
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The marker approach is similar to how tools like Prettier manage sections in config files. Read the file, find markers, replace content between them.

userStories.title: Implement CodexAdapter and GeminiAdapter
userStories.description: As a developer, I need adapters for Codex and Gemini CLI to convert skills to their native formats.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/cli/src/adapters/codex.ts: reads/writes .codex/config.toml, adds [skills.<name>] sections with description and instructions
CodexAdapter.remove() removes the [skills.<name>] section from TOML
CodexAdapter preserves all other TOML content
Creates .codex/config.toml if it doesn't exist
Create apps/cli/src/adapters/gemini.ts: writes .gemini/commands/<skill-name>.toml with skill metadata
GeminiAdapter also manages <!-- my-skills:start/end --> markers in GEMINI.md for skill references
GeminiAdapter.remove() deletes the TOML file and removes reference from GEMINI.md
Creates .gemini/commands/ directory and GEMINI.md if needed
Add smol-toml (or @iarna/toml) as dependency for TOML parsing
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Codex uses TOML config, Gemini uses TOML command files. Both need a TOML parser. smol-toml is a lightweight option.

userStories.title: Integrate adapters into add/remove commands
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the add and remove commands to invoke agent adapters after skill installation.
acceptanceCriteria: After installing a skill via add command, run adapter.install() for each enabled agent
After removing a skill via remove command, run adapter.remove() for each enabled agent
If no agents configured yet, trigger agent detection flow (from US-020) before running adapters
Log which adapters were invoked (e.g., 'Deployed to: claude-code, cursor, github-copilot')
Adapter failures are logged as warnings but don't fail the overall command
Store the agents list in the manifest entry for each installed skill
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This wires together the adapter system with the existing add/remove commands. Use getEnabledAdapters() from the registry.

userStories.title: Implement update command
userStories.description: As a developer, I want ms update to update installed skills to their latest versions.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/cli/src/commands/update.ts registered with alias 'up'
ms update (no args) updates all installed skills from manifest
ms update <skill-name> updates a specific skill
For each skill: fetches latest from remote via cache, computes new hash, compares with manifest
If hash differs: removes old files, installs new version, re-runs adapter install, updates manifest entry (hash, updatedAt)
If hash matches: prints 'skill-name: already up to date'
Shows summary: N updated, N already up-to-date, N failed
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Similar to add but overwrites existing installations. Force-fetches from remote (ignores cache staleness).

userStories.title: Implement check command
userStories.description: As a developer, I want ms check to see which skills have updates available.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/cli/src/commands/check.ts
Reads manifest, for each skill: fetches latest from remote, computes hash, compares
Displays table: skill name, status (up-to-date / update available / remote unavailable), current hash (8 chars), latest hash (8 chars)
Shows 'No skills installed' if manifest is empty
Shows spinner during fetch operations
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: check is read-only - it reports status but doesn't modify anything. Use the cache service with forced fetch.

userStories.title: Implement find command with favorites search
userStories.description: As a developer, I want ms find to search for skills across my favorites and installed skills.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/cli/src/commands/find.ts
ms find (no query) launches @inquirer/search interactive fuzzy-find
ms find <query> pre-fills the search with the query string
Search index includes: installed skills (from manifest) + all skills from cached favorite repos
Favorite repos fetched if not cached or if cache is stale (1 hour TTL)
Each search result shows: skill name, description, source repo, '[installed]' badge if already installed
Fuzzy match against name and description
Selecting a result prompts: 'Install skill-name from owner/repo?' then delegates to add flow
Reads favoriteRepos from global config
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The find command is the discovery tool. It aggregates skills from all favorite repos + installed skills into one searchable list.

userStories.title: Implement init command
userStories.description: As a developer, I want ms init to scaffold a new SKILL.md.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/cli/src/commands/init.ts
ms init <name> creates .agents/skills/<name>/SKILL.md with frontmatter template
ms init (no name) prompts for name using @inquirer/input
Prompts for description using @inquirer/input
Generated SKILL.md has --- delimited YAML frontmatter with name and description, plus placeholder markdown body
Creates directory .agents/skills/<name>/ if it doesn't exist
Shows error if skill already exists (with suggestion to use --force)
--force flag overwrites existing skill
Shows success message with path to created file
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The generated SKILL.md should follow the agent skills spec format with name and description frontmatter.

userStories.title: Implement config command
userStories.description: As a developer, I want ms config to manage global configuration.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/cli/src/commands/config.ts with subcommands: get, set, list, delete
ms config get <key>: reads and prints a config value (supports dot-notation for nested values)
ms config set <key> <value>: sets a value, validates with ConfigSchema, saves
ms config set defaultAgents claude-code,cursor handles comma-separated arrays
ms config list: pretty-prints all config key-value pairs with chalk formatting
ms config delete <key>: removes key (resets to default), saves
All operations read from / write to ~/.my-skills/config.json
Shows current value after set operations
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Model after npm config. Use Commander subcommands for get/set/list/delete.

userStories.title: Scaffold Next.js web app with tRPC
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Next.js app wired up with tRPC and Drizzle so we can build frontend features.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/web/ exists (rename from apps/nextjs/ if needed) as the Next.js app
Update package.json name and all imports/references from nextjs to web
tRPC client configured in apps/web/ connecting to @curiouslycory/api
REPO_PATH env var read for filesystem operations (defaults to process.cwd())
Workspace dependencies: @curiouslycory/shared-types, @curiouslycory/db, @curiouslycory/git-service, @curiouslycory/api, @curiouslycory/ui
pnpm dev --filter @curiouslycory/web starts dev server without errors
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The create-t3-turbo scaffold already has a Next.js app with tRPC. Rename it from nextjs to web and ensure the wiring works with the modified db package.

userStories.title: Implement auth package
userStories.description: As a developer, I need basic auth so the deployed web app can be password-protected.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/auth/ exists as @curiouslycory/auth (modify existing create-t3-turbo auth package)
Replace Better Auth with simple cookie-based JWT auth
validate(username, password) checks against ADMIN_USER and ADMIN_PASSWORD env vars
createSession(username) returns a signed JWT using AUTH_SECRET env var
verifySession(token) validates JWT and returns decoded payload or null
isAuthEnabled() returns true only when ADMIN_USER env var is set
When auth is disabled (no ADMIN_USER), all requests are treated as authenticated
Uses jose library for JWT operations
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Replace the Better Auth setup from create-t3-turbo with this simpler approach. Auth is optional - disabled by default for local dev.

userStories.title: Implement skill tRPC router
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the skill CRUD API so the frontend can manage skills.
acceptanceCriteria: Create skill router in packages/api/src/router/skill.ts
skill.list: query returning all skills from DB with optional category and tags filters
skill.byId: query returning single skill by ID with full content
skill.create: mutation that writes SKILL.md to skills/<name>/ on disk AND inserts into DB
skill.update: mutation that updates SKILL.md on disk AND updates DB record
skill.delete: mutation that removes skill directory AND deletes DB record
skill.syncFromDisk: mutation that scans skills/ directory, parses SKILL.md frontmatter, upserts into DB, removes stale DB entries
All inputs validated with Zod schemas
Create and update use protected procedures (auth required when enabled)
Register skill router in the app router
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The skill router is the primary API for the web app. syncFromDisk is called on startup and after git pull to keep the DB in sync with the filesystem.

userStories.title: Implement artifact tRPC router
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the artifact CRUD API for agents, prompts, and claudemds.
acceptanceCriteria: Create artifact router in packages/api/src/router/artifact.ts
artifact.list: query returning all artifacts with optional category filter
artifact.listByCategory: query filtering by category enum (agent/prompt/claudemd)
artifact.byId: query returning single artifact with full content
artifact.create: mutation writing to artifacts/<category>/<name>/ on disk + DB
artifact.update: mutation updating on disk + DB
artifact.delete: mutation removing directory + DB record
artifact.syncFromDisk: mutation scanning all artifacts/ subdirectories, upserting to DB
All mutations use protected procedures
Register artifact router in the app router
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Same pattern as skill router but for artifacts directory. Category determines subdirectory: artifacts/agents/, artifacts/prompts/, artifacts/claudemds/.

userStories.title: Implement disk sync service
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a service that syncs the filesystem with the database on startup.
acceptanceCriteria: Create packages/api/src/lib/disk-sync.ts with scanAndSync(repoPath, db) function
Walks skills/*/SKILL.md parsing frontmatter with gray-matter
Walks artifacts/*/*/SKILL.md (or metadata.json) parsing metadata
Upserts each found item into the skills DB table matched on dirPath
Removes DB records whose dirPath no longer exists on disk
Returns summary: { added: number, updated: number, removed: number }
Called from a tRPC procedure (skill.syncFromDisk and artifact.syncFromDisk should use this)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the bridge between filesystem (source of truth) and database (search index). Use recursive directory walking.

userStories.title: Implement search tRPC router with FTS5
userStories.description: As a developer, I need full-text search so the web app can find skills and artifacts.
acceptanceCriteria: Create search router in packages/api/src/router/search.ts
search.query procedure: accepts query string, optional category filter, limit (default 20), offset
Uses FTS5 MATCH query with prefix matching (append * to each search term)
Returns results with highlighted snippets using FTS5 snippet() function
Results ordered by FTS5 rank for relevance
Handles empty query gracefully (returns recent items ordered by updatedAt)
Register search router in the app router
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: FTS5 queries use raw SQL via db.run(sql`...`). The snippet() function wraps matches in <mark> tags. Initialize FTS tables (from US-006) on app startup.

userStories.title: Implement git tRPC router
userStories.description: As a developer, I need git operations API for the web UI.
acceptanceCriteria: Create git router in packages/api/src/router/git.ts
git.status: query returning current branch, clean/dirty, list of modified files
git.log: query returning recent commits with hash, message, author, date (supports path filter and pagination)
git.diff: query returning diff for a specific commit or working tree changes
git.commit: mutation staging specified files and creating commit
git.push: mutation pushing current branch to origin
git.pull: mutation pulling from origin
git.fetch: mutation fetching from origin
git.branches: query listing all branches with current branch indicator
git.checkout: mutation switching to a branch
All mutations use protected procedures
Uses @curiouslycory/git-service instantiated with repoPath from context
Register git router in the app router
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The git router wraps the git-service package. repoPath comes from the tRPC context (REPO_PATH env var or cwd).

userStories.title: Implement config and favorites tRPC router
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the config API for managing settings and favorites from the web UI.
acceptanceCriteria: Create config router in packages/api/src/router/config.ts
config.get: query returning config value by key from DB config table
config.getAll: query returning all config entries
config.set: mutation upserting config value in DB
config.favorites.list: query returning all favorites from DB
config.favorites.add: mutation inserting into favorites table
config.favorites.remove: mutation deleting from favorites table
All mutations use protected procedures
Register config router in the app router
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Config and favorites are stored in the DB for the web app. They get synced to ~/.my-skills/config.json for CLI access (sync implemented in a later story).

acceptanceCriteria: Create auth router in packages/api/src/router/auth.ts
auth.login: mutation validating credentials and returning session token (sets httpOnly cookie)
auth.logout: mutation clearing session cookie
auth.session: query returning current session state (authenticated or not)
Create apps/web/src/app/(auth)/login/page.tsx with username/password form
Login form calls auth.login mutation, redirects to / on success
Create middleware.ts that redirects unauthenticated users to /login when auth is enabled
Auth is completely bypassed when ADMIN_USER env var is not set
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev server
userStories.notes: Use the @curiouslycory/auth package for JWT operations. The middleware reads the session cookie and verifies it.

Header with app title 'my-skills' and global search input
Active nav item is visually highlighted based on current route
Search input navigates to /search?q=<query> on submit (Enter key)
Sidebar is responsive: collapses to hamburger menu on mobile (<768px)

userStories.title: Build skill detail page with version history

Renders SKILL.md body as formatted markdown with syntax highlighting (use remark/rehype or similar)
Metadata sidebar: name, description, tags as badges, author, version, category
Edit button linking to /skills/[id]/edit
Delete button with confirmation dialog (calls skill.delete mutation)
Version history section below content showing git log for the skill's directory (calls git.log with path filter)
Each commit entry shows: hash (8 chars), message, author, relative date

userStories.title: Implement Plate.js editor component
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a reusable Plate.js WYSIWYG editor for editing SKILL.md files.
acceptanceCriteria: Install @udecode/plate, @udecode/plate-markdown, and required plugins
Create apps/web/src/app/_components/plate-editor.tsx as a 'use client' component
Configure Plate with plugins: paragraph, heading, bold, italic, code, code block, link, list, blockquote
Markdown serialization via @udecode/plate-markdown (deserialize markdown string to Plate value, serialize Plate value back to markdown string)
Editor toolbar with formatting buttons (bold, italic, heading levels, code, link, list)
Component accepts initialContent (markdown string) and onSave callback
Lazy loaded via next/dynamic to minimize bundle impact
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev server
userStories.notes: Plate.js is a rich-text editor built on Slate. The markdown plugin handles the serialization round-trip. This component is reused by skill edit, artifact edit, and composition preview.

userStories.title: Build frontmatter form component
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a form component for editing SKILL.md frontmatter fields.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/web/src/app/_components/frontmatter-form.tsx as a 'use client' component
Form fields: name (text input), description (textarea), tags (multi-input with add/remove), author (text input), version (text input)
Tags field allows typing a tag and pressing Enter to add it, clicking X to remove
Form accepts initial values and onChange callback that emits the full frontmatter object
Uses shadcn/ui Input, Textarea, Badge, Button components
Form validates name is non-empty
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev server
userStories.notes: This is the top section of the skill/artifact edit page. The Plate editor sits below it for the body content.

userStories.title: Build skill create and edit pages
userStories.description: As a user, I want to create and edit skills through the web UI.
acceptanceCriteria: /skills/new page with FrontmatterForm + Plate.js editor
Save button calls skill.create mutation: assembles YAML frontmatter from form + serialized markdown from Plate editor, writes to disk + DB
/skills/[id]/edit page loads existing skill: gray-matter splits frontmatter -> form fields, body -> Plate deserializer
Save button calls skill.update mutation
After save, shows toast confirmation and redirects to skill detail page
Cancel button returns to previous page
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev server
userStories.notes: The create page assembles a new SKILL.md. The edit page loads an existing one. Both use the same FrontmatterForm + PlateEditor components.

userStories.title: Build artifact browse, detail, and edit pages
userStories.description: As a user, I want to browse, view, and edit artifacts (agents, prompts, claudemds).

Tab selection filters the list via artifact.listByCategory query
/artifacts/[category]/[id] shows artifact detail (same layout as skill detail page)
/artifacts/[category]/[id]/edit uses FrontmatterForm + Plate.js editor
/artifacts/[category]/new for creating new artifacts in a specific category
Delete with confirmation dialog on detail page

userStories.title: Build search results page
userStories.description: As a user, I want to search and see highlighted results.
acceptanceCriteria: Create /search page (apps/web/src/app/search/page.tsx)
Reads ?q= URL param and pre-fills search input
Debounced search (300ms) calls search.query tRPC procedure
Results show: name, category badge, description, content snippet with <mark> highlighted matches
Results link to the appropriate detail page (skill or artifact)
Optional category filter dropdown on the page
Shows 'No results found' for empty result sets
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev server
userStories.notes: The FTS5 snippet() function returns HTML with highlighted matches. Render carefully to avoid XSS (sanitize or use dangerouslySetInnerHTML on trusted content only).

userStories.title: Implement heading merge algorithm
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the merge algorithm for composing multi-fragment CLAUDE.md files.
acceptanceCriteria: Create packages/api/src/lib/merge.ts with mergeFragments(fragments: string[]): string
Parses each fragment into heading-tree sections by splitting on /^(#{1,6})\s+(.+)$/m
Matches headings by normalized text: lowercase, trimmed, compared across fragments
Matched headings: concatenate body content in fragment order under single heading
Deduplicate identical lines (after trim) within merged sections
Level-offset matching: # Foo in fragment A matches ## Foo in fragment B if structural pattern matches (same relative children). Use the level from the first occurrence.
Unmatched headings: insert at best structural position (closest matching parent chain)
Multiple structural matches: merge into first match and stop
Preamble (content before first heading): concatenated, identical lines deduplicated
Post-process: collapse 3+ consecutive blank lines to 2, ensure trailing newline
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the core algorithm for the composition builder. It must be a pure function for easy testing. The level-offset matching is the most complex part - see the PRD for detailed examples.

userStories.title: Implement merge algorithm unit tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I need comprehensive tests for the heading merge algorithm.
acceptanceCriteria: Create packages/api/src/lib/__tests__/merge.test.ts using vitest
Test: single fragment returns unchanged
Test: two fragments with no overlapping headings concatenates them
Test: two fragments with same ## heading merges content under one heading
Test: level-offset matching (# Foo and ## Foo with same structure merge correctly)
Test: duplicate identical lines within merged section are deduplicated
Test: fragment ordering is preserved (first fragment's content appears first)
Test: preamble content is merged and deduplicated
Test: 3+ blank lines collapsed to 2
Test: empty fragments array returns empty string
Test: complex scenario from PRD with 3 files merging at different heading levels
All tests pass
userStories.notes: Use the exact examples from the PRD (File 1, File 2, File 3 and expected combined result) as test cases.

userStories.title: Implement composition tRPC router
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the composition API for the composition builder UI.
acceptanceCriteria: Create composition router in packages/api/src/router/composition.ts
composition.list: query returning all saved compositions from DB
composition.byId: query returning single composition with resolved fragment content (joins with skills table)
composition.create: mutation saving name, description, fragment IDs, order to DB
composition.update: mutation updating composition
composition.delete: mutation removing composition
composition.preview: query accepting fragment IDs + order, fetches content from DB, runs mergeFragments(), returns merged markdown string
composition.exportMarkdown: query returning final merged markdown
All mutations use protected procedures
Register composition router in the app router
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The preview endpoint is called frequently (on every selection change) so it should be efficient. Fragment content is fetched from the skills DB table.

userStories.title: Build composition builder - fragment selection table
userStories.description: As a user, I want to select fragments from a data table to compose a CLAUDE.md file.
acceptanceCriteria: Create /compositions/new page with data table of all claudemd artifacts
Table columns: checkbox, name, description, tags (as badges), updated date
Checkbox multi-select with 'select all' header checkbox
Selected fragments appear in a sidebar panel on the right showing current selection
Sidebar shows fragment count and 'Clear all' button
Uses shadcn/ui DataTable pattern with checkboxes
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev server
userStories.notes: This is the left panel of the composition builder. The right panel (preview) is added in a subsequent story.

userStories.title: Build composition builder - drag-and-drop ordering
userStories.description: As a user, I want to reorder selected fragments via drag-and-drop.

Selected fragments in the sidebar have drag handles (grip icon)
Drag-and-drop reordering using DndContext + SortableContext from @dnd-kit
Order changes update the fragment list state immediately
Deselecting a fragment (unchecking in table) removes it from the order list
Fragment order is used when calling composition.preview
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev server
userStories.notes: The ordering determines the merge order for the heading merge algorithm. First fragment takes precedence for heading levels.

userStories.title: Build composition builder - live preview panel
